Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code, the Canada Evidence Act and the Sex Offender Information Registration Act, to enact the High Risk Child Sex Offender Database Act and to make consequential amendments to other Acts
Identification.ShortTitle: Tougher Penalties for Child Predators Act
Identification.RunningHead: Criminal Code, Canada Evidence, Sex Offender Information Registration and High Risk Child Sex Offender Database

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: increase mandatory minimum penalties and maximum penalties for certain sexual offences against children;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: increase maximum penalties for violations of prohibition orders, probation orders and peace bonds;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: clarify and codify the rules regarding the imposition of consecutive and concurrent sentences;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: require courts to impose, in certain cases, consecutive sentences on offenders who commit sexual offences against children; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: ensure that a court that imposes a sentence must take into consideration evidence that the offence in question was committed while the offender was subject to a conditional sentence order or released on parole, statutory release or unescorted temporary absence.
It amends the Canada Evidence Act to ensure that spouses of the accused are competent and compellable witnesses for the prosecution in child pornography cases.
It also amends the Sex Offender Information Registration Act to increase the reporting obligations of sex offenders who travel outside Canada.

It enacts the High Risk Child Sex Offender Database Act to establish a publicly accessible database that contains information — that a police service or other public authority has previously made accessible to the public — with respect to persons who are found guilty of sexual offences against children and who pose a high risk of committing crimes of a sexual nature.
Finally, it makes consequential amendments to other Acts.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Tougher Penalties for Child Predators Act.
Paragraphs 151(a) and (b) of the Criminal Code are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of 90 days.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 152(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of 90 days.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 153(1.1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of 90 days.

Section.Text: Paragraph 160(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Section.Text: Paragraphs 161(4)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than four years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 18 months.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 163.1(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Every person who makes, prints, publishes or possesses for the purpose of publication any child pornography is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.

Every person who transmits, makes available, distributes, sells, advertises, imports, exports or possesses for the purpose of transmission, making available, distribution, sale, advertising or exportation any child pornography is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.
Paragraphs 163.1(4)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of six months.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 163.1(4.1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of six months.

Section.Text: Section 170 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Parent or guardian procuring sexual activity
Section.Text: Every parent or guardian of a person under the age of 18 years who procures the person for the purpose of engaging in any sexual activity prohibited by this Act with a person other than the parent or guardian is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.
Section 171 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every owner, occupier or manager of premises, or any other person who has control of premises or assists in the management or control of premises, who knowingly permits a person under the age of 18 years to resort to or to be in or on the premises for the purpose of engaging in any sexual activity prohibited by this Act is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year.
Paragraphs 171.1(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of six months; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of 90 days.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 172.1(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of six months.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 172.2(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of six months.

Section.Text: Subsection 212(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who, in any place, obtains for consideration, or communicates with anyone for the purpose of obtaining for consideration, the sexual services of a person who is under the age of 18 years is guilty of an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a first offence, six months; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a second or subsequent offence, one year.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (4), the only question to be considered is the sequence of convictions and no consideration shall be given to the sequence of commission of offences or whether any offence occurred before or after any conviction.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 271(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years or, if the complainant is under the age of 16 years, to imprisonment for a term of not more than 14 years and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of one year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 18 months or, if the complainant is under the age of 16 years, to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years less a day and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of six months.

Section.Text: Paragraph 272(2)(a.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: if the complainant is under the age of 16 years, to imprisonment for life and to a minimum punishment of imprisonment for a term of five years; and

Section.Text: Paragraph 718.2(a) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (iv), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (v) and by adding the following after subparagraph (v):

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: evidence that the offence was committed while the offender was subject to a conditional sentence order made under section 742.1 or released on parole, statutory release or unescorted temporary absence under the Corrections and Conditional Release Act

Section.Text: Subsection 718.3(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The court that sentences an accused shall consider directing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the term of imprisonment that it imposes be served consecutively to a sentence of imprisonment to which the accused is subject at the time of sentencing; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the terms of imprisonment that it imposes at the same time for more than one offence be served consecutively, including when

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the offences do not arise out of the same event or series of events,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: one of the offences was committed while the accused was on judicial interim release, including pending the determination of an appeal, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: one of the offences was committed while the accused was fleeing from a peace officer.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (4), a term of imprisonment includes imprisonment that results from the operation of subsection 734(4).
For the purposes of subsection (4), a sentence of imprisonment includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a disposition made under paragraph 20(1)(k) or (k.1) of the Young Offenders Act, chapter Y-1 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1985;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a youth sentence imposed under paragraph 42(2)(n), (o), (q) or (r) of the Youth Criminal Justice Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a sentence that results from the operation of subsection 743.5(1) or (2).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Cumulative punishments — sexual offences against children
Subsection.Text: When a court sentences an accused at the same time for more than one sexual offence committed against a child, the court shall direct

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that a sentence of imprisonment it imposes for an offence under section 163.1 be served consecutively to a sentence of imprisonment it imposes for a sexual offence under another section of this Act committed against a child; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that a sentence of imprisonment it imposes for a sexual offence committed against a child, other than an offence under section 163.1, be served consecutively to a sentence of imprisonment it imposes for a sexual offence committed against another child other than an offence under section 163.1.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 733.1(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than four years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 18 months, or to a fine of not more than $5000, or to both.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 811(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than four years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 18 months.

Section.Text: Subsection 4(2) of the Canada Evidence Act is amended by replacing “170” with “163.1, 170”.
Subsection 3(1) of the Sex Offender Information Registration Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: sexual offence against a child means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a designated offence as defined in subsection 490.011(1) of the Criminal Code that is committed against a person who is under 18 years of age and as a result of which the offender is required to comply with this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence that is committed outside Canada against a person who is under 18 years of age and as a result of which the offender is required to comply with this Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 4.1(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: within seven days after they receive a driver’s licence or, if they are required to report to a registration centre designated under the National Defence Act, within 15 days after they receive it;

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: within seven days after they receive a passport or, if they are required to report to a registration centre designated under the National Defence Act, within 15 days after they receive it; and

Section.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (g) and by adding the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the licence number and the name of the issuing jurisdiction of every driver’s licence that they hold; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the passport number and the name of the issuing jurisdiction of every passport that they hold.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 6(1) of the Act before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (1.1), a sex offender other than one who is referred to in subsection (1.01) shall notify a person who collects information at the registration centre referred to in section 7.1

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before the sex offender’s departure — of the dates of their departure and return and of every address or location at which they expect to stay in Canada or outside Canada — if they expect not to be at their main residence or any of their secondary residences for a period of seven or more consecutive days;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: within seven days after their departure — of the date of their return and of every address or location at which they are staying in Canada or outside Canada — if they decide, after departure, not to be at their main residence or any of their secondary residences for a period of seven or more consecutive days or if they have not given a notification required under paragraph (a); and

Subsection.Text: Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Sex offender convicted of sex offence against child
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.1), a sex offender who is convicted of a sexual offence against a child shall notify a person who collects information at the registration centre referred to in section 7.1

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before the sex offender’s departure — of the dates of their departure and return and of every address or location at which they expect to stay in Canada — if they expect not to be at their main residence or any of their secondary residences for a period of seven or more consecutive days;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: before their departure, of the dates of their departure and return and of every address or location at which they expect to stay outside Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: within seven days after their departure — of the date of their return and of every address or location at which they are staying in Canada — if they decide, after departure, not to be at their main residence or any of their secondary residences for a period of seven or more consecutive days or if they have not given a notification required under paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: without delay, after their departure — of the date of their return and of every address or location at which they are staying outside Canada — if they decide, after departure, to extend their stay beyond the date of return that they indicated in the notification they gave under paragraph (b) or if they have not given a notification under paragraph (b); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: of a change in address, location or date, before their departure or

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the change is made after their departure and they are staying in Canada, within seven days after the date on which the change is made, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the change is made after their departure and they are staying outside Canada, without delay after the date on which the change is made.

Section.Text: Section 8.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Registration of information — Canada Border Services Agency
Subsection.Text: A person who registers information for the Commissioner of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police may register in the database the information disclosed to the Commissioner under subsection 15.2(2).

Section.Text: The heading before section 15.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: AUTHORITY TO COLLECT OR DISCLOSE INFORMATION

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 15.1:

Section.MarginalNote: Canada Border Services Agency — collection of information

Subsection.Text: The Canada Border Services Agency may assist a member or an employee of, or a person retained by, a police service in the prevention or investigation of a crime of a sexual nature or an offence under section 490.031 or 490.0311 of the Criminal Code or in the laying of a charge for such an offence by collecting the information disclosed to it under paragraph 16(4)(j.2) or (j.3) as well as the following information with respect to any sex offender who is the subject of a disclosure made under those paragraphs:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the date of their departure from Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the date of their return to Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: every address or location at which they have stayed outside Canada.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Canada Border Services Agency — disclosure of information
Subsection.Text: The Canada Border Services Agency may, in assisting the member or employee of, or person retained by, a police service referred to in subsection (1), disclose to the Commissioner of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police any information collected under paragraphs (1)(a) to (c).
Subsection 16(3) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: the information was collected under subsection 15.2(1) and they compare it with other information for the purpose of assisting a member or an employee of, or a person retained by, a police service in the prevention or investigation of a crime of a sexual nature or an offence under section 490.031 or 490.0311 of the Criminal Code or in the laying of a charge for such an offence; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 16(3.1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a.2) and by adding the following after paragraph (a.2):

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: the information was collected under subsection 15.2(1) and they combine it with information contained in a computer system of the Canada Border Services Agency for the purpose of assisting a member or an employee of, or a person retained by, a police service in the prevention or investigation of a crime of a sexual nature or an offence under section 490.031 or 490.0311 of the Criminal Code or in the laying of a charge for such an offence; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 16(4) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (j.1) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (j.2)
Paragraph.Text: unless the disclosure is to the Canada Border Services Agency, is limited to the information referred to in paragraphs 5(1)(a), (b), (i) and (j) and is necessary to assist a member or an employee of, or a person retained by, a police service in the prevention or investigation of a crime of a sexual nature or an offence under section 490.031 or 490.0311 of the Criminal Code or in the laying of a charge for such an offence;

Paragraph.Label: (j.3)
Paragraph.Text: unless the disclosure is to the Canada Border Services Agency, relates to a sex offender who is convicted of a sexual offence against a child and who poses a high risk of committing a crime of a sexual nature, is limited to the information referred to in paragraphs 5(1)(a), (b), (i) and (j) and is made for the purpose of assisting a member or an employee of, or a person retained by, a police service in the prevention or investigation of a crime of a sexual nature or an offence under section 490.031 or 490.0311 of the Criminal Code or in the laying of a charge for such an offence; or

TitleText.XRefExternal: HIGH RISK CHILD SEX OFFENDER DATABASE ACT

Section.Text: The High Risk Child Sex Offender Database Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act respecting the establishment of a publicly accessible database containing information with respect to persons who are found guilty of sexual offences against children and who pose a high risk of committing crimes of a sexual nature

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the High Risk Child Sex Offender Database Act.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: sexual offence against a child means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a designated offence as defined in subsection 490.011(1) of the Criminal Code that is committed against a person who is under 18 years of age, other than an offence referred to in paragraph (b) or (f) of the definition designated offence in that subsection if the prosecutor has not, for the purposes of subsection 490.012(2) of that Act, established beyond a reasonable doubt that the offender committed the offence with the intent to commit an offence referred to in paragraph (a), (c), (c.1), (d), (d.1) or (e) of that definition; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence that is committed outside Canada against a person who is under 18 years of age and as a result of which the offender is or was required to comply with the Sex Offender Information Registration Act.

Definition.Text: Commissioner means the Commissioner of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interpretation— crime of a sexual nature
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a crime is of a sexual nature if it consists of one or more acts that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are either sexual in nature or committed with the intent to commit an act or acts that are sexual in nature; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: constitute an offence.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a reference to a person who is found guilty of a sexual offence against a child does not include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a young person as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Youth Criminal Justice Act who has been found guilty of a sexual offence against a child unless they are given an adult sentence as defined in that subsection for the offence; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a young person as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Young Offenders Act, chapter Y-1 of the Revised Statutes of Canada, 1985, who has been found guilty of a sexual offence against a child unless they are found guilty of the offence in ordinary court as defined in that subsection.

Section.Text: The purpose of this Act is to enhance public safety by establishing a publicly accessible database that contains information with respect to persons who are found guilty of sexual offences against children and who pose a high risk of committing crimes of a sexual nature.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner must establish and administer a publicly accessible database that contains information with respect to persons who are found guilty of sexual offences against children and who pose a high risk of committing crimes of a sexual nature.
The Commissioner’s duties under this Act may be performed on behalf of the Commissioner by any person who is authorized by the Commissioner to perform those duties.

Section.Text: The database must contain only information, with respect to any person referred to in subsection 4(1), that a police service or other public authority has previously made accessible to the public, including:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: their given name and surname, and any alias that they use;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: their date of birth;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: their gender;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: their physical description, including any physical distinguishing mark that they have;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a photograph of them;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a description of offences they have committed;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any condition to which they are subject; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the city, town, municipality or other organized district in which they reside.

Section.Text: The Commissioner must, before including the given name and surname of a person referred to in subsection 4(1) in the database, take reasonable steps to notify that person that information with respect to them is to be included in the database and allow that person to make representations concerning the matter.

Subsection.Text: On the receipt of an application in writing by a person referred to in subsection 4(1), the Commissioner must decide whether there are reasonable grounds to remove information with respect to that person from the database.
If the Commissioner does not make a decision on the application within the period prescribed by regulation, the Commissioner is deemed to have decided not to remove the information in question.
The Commissioner must give notice without delay to the applicant of any decision made or deemed to have been made respecting the application.
Within 60 days after receipt of the notice of the decision, the applicant may apply to the Federal Court for judicial review of the decision.

Section.Text: Within the period prescribed by regulation after the establishment of the database, and at prescribed intervals after that, the Commissioner must review the information in the database to determine whether the information should still be maintained in the database. The review does not affect the validity of the database.
Nothing in this Act affects any authority of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police under another Act of Parliament or an Act of a provincial legislature or at common law to disclose information.

No criminal or civil proceedings lie against the Commissioner, or against any person acting on behalf or under the direction of the Commissioner, for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith under this Act.
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establishing the criteria for determining whether a person who is found guilty of a sexual offence against a child poses a high risk of committing a crime of a sexual nature; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that, by this Act, is to be prescribed.

Section.Text: Subsection 490.031(3) of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Proof of certain facts by certificate
Subsection.Text: In proceedings under subsection (1), a certificate of a person referred to in paragraph 16(2)(b) of the Sex Offender Information Registration Act stating that the sex offender failed to report under section 4, 4.1, 4.2 or 4.3 — or provide information under section 5 or notify a person under subsection 6(1) or (1.01) — of that Act is evidence of the statements contained in it without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed it.

Section.Text: The portion of section 490.0311 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every person who knowingly provides false or misleading information under subsection 5(1) or 6(1) or (1.01) of the Sex Offender Information Registration Act is guilty of an offence and liable
Subsection 119.1(3) of the National Defence Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Proof of certain facts by certificate
Subsection.Text: In proceedings under subsection (1), a certificate of a person referred to in paragraph 16(2)(b.1) of the Sex Offender Information Registration Act stating that the person named in the certificate failed to report under section 4, 4.1, 4.2 or 4.3 — or provide information under section 5 or notify a person under subsection 6(1) or (1.01) — of that Act is evidence of the statements contained in it without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed it.

Section.Text: If Bill C-13, introduced in the 2nd session of the 41st Parliament and entitled the Protecting Canadians from Online Crime Act, receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both section 3 of that Act and section 6 of this Act are in force, paragraphs 162.2(4)(a) and (b) of the Criminal Code are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than four years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and is liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than 18 months.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Act, other than section 33, come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
